title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which is due by January 31st. After self-assessments are submitted, managers will review them and schedule one-on-one meetings to discuss performance, set goals, and provide feedback. These meetings usually take place in February. To effectively complete your self-assessment, reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for growth over the past year. Use specific examples to illustrate your contributions and align them with company values and goals. If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out to your HR representative or your manager for guidance. Remember, the self-assessment is an opportunity to advocate for your professional development and career aspirations.
